landing.heroTitle: The world has always saved in circles.
landing.heroNames: Somiti. Tanda. Susu. Hui. Chit fund. Paluwagan. Gam'eya.
landing.heroSubtitle: Rota puts them on rails — smart-contract savings circles in digital dollars, with no organizer who can run away with the pot.
landing.howStep1Desc: Invite 3–20 people you save with — family, coworkers, your street, your diaspora.
landing.howStep2Desc: A fixed USDC amount each round, held by the smart contract — not by any person.
landing.howStep3Title: Each round, one member takes the pot
landing.howStep3Desc: By fixed order, by draw, or by bidding. After a full cycle, everyone has had their turn.
landing.productCircleDesc: The classic rotating circle. Fixed contributions, one pot per round, everyone gets a turn.
landing.productBidDesc: Need money sooner? Bid a discount to take the pot early — the discount becomes a dividend for everyone else.
landing.productPotDesc: Save together toward a wedding, tuition, Eid, or land. Locked until you reach the goal; everyone withdraws their own money.
landing.trustEscrowDesc: Funds sit in an audited-pattern smart contract. Nobody — not even the organizer — can take them.
landing.trustAutoDesc: Rounds settle deterministically. Collateral covers missed payments.
landing.trustBorderlessDesc: A garment worker in Dhaka, her brother in Dubai, a cousin in London — one circle, remittance and savings merged.
landing.trustReputationDesc: Every on-time contribution builds an on-chain credit history — portable proof for the world's unbanked.
landing.corridorHeadlineSub: "Your turn always comes" — the somiti your family already trusts, now with contract-grade guarantees.
landing.corridorExampleDesc: Rahima in Dhaka, her brother Karim in Dubai, and cousin Nadia in London save 100 USDC a week together. Every payout lands instantly — no remittance fees, no waiting at the counter.
landing.corridorNote: Bangla interface, taka amounts on every screen, and diaspora corridors from Dubai, KSA, Malaysia, the UK and the US.
landing.testnetNote: Rota runs on Arc testnet. Testnet USDC only — not real money, not an offer of financial services.

dashboard.title: Your savings
dashboard.emptyCta: Create your first circle — it takes two minutes.
dashboard.notMemberHint: Circles you join with this wallet will appear here.

create.title: Create
create.membersHelp: 3 to 20. The circle runs one round per member.
create.modeRandomHelp: Drawn at start (on-chain randomness, see docs).
create.modeBidHelp: Members bid a discount to take the pot early.
create.collateralHelp: Held as security and slashed if someone misses a round. 100% = one full contribution.
create.bidWindowHelp: The first part of each round when bids are accepted.
create.maxDiscountHelp: Caps how much of the pot a desperate bidder can give up (max 30%).
create.openDeadlineHelp: If the circle isn't full by then, collateral can be reclaimed.
create.inviteOnlyHelp: Only addresses you approve can join.
create.givingToggle: Route a share of each pot to charity
create.deadlineHelp: The pot unlocks when the target is reached or the deadline passes.
create.haircutHelp: Kept from anyone who leaves early and shared among those who stay.
create.reviewNote: Check everything — circle rules cannot be changed after creation.
create.inviteHint: Share this link — each member connects a wallet and joins with one tap.

circle.joinCollateralNote: Joining escrows {{amount}} collateral, returned when the circle completes.
circle.activateHint: All members are in. Anyone can start it.
circle.autopayExplain: Let anyone trigger your exact contribution each round — set and forget, without giving custody. The contract can only ever pull {{amount}} once per round while you stay opted in.
circle.autopayAllowanceWarning: Your allowance covers {{rounds}} more rounds. Increase it so AutoPay doesn't fail later.
circle.bidNone: No bids yet — the pot goes by join order if nobody bids.
circle.bidExplain: Bid the share of the pot you'd give up to take it now. The discount is split among all members as a dividend.
circle.settleHint: Everyone has paid (or the deadline passed) — anyone can settle.
circle.cureExplain: Repays what your collateral covered plus a 5% penalty, restoring your standing and payout eligibility.
circle.cancelHint: Circle never filled — cancel and reclaim collateral.
circle.approveNote: First approve {{amount}} USDC, then confirm the action.
circle.youAreNext: You're next in line for the pot!
circle.notInvitedNote: This circle is invite-only. Ask the organizer to allowlist your address.

pot.bonusNote: Includes your share of early-exit penalties.
pot.emergencyWarning: Leaving before unlock costs you {{percent}} of your balance, shared among members who stay. This is recorded on your Credit Passport.

passport.title: Credit Passport
passport.subtitle: Portable, on-chain proof of savings discipline.
passport.formula: score = completions×100 + contributions×10 + cures×20 − defaults×50 − earlyExits×15
passport.shareHint: Show this to a lender, landlord, or employer — anyone can verify it on-chain.
passport.verifyFailed: Verification failed — the signature didn't match this address. Make sure the connected wallet is the one shown on this passport, then try again.
passport.verifyExplain: Signs a message with your wallet (free, off-chain) proving you control this passport.
passport.noHistory: No activity yet. Join a circle to start building your score.

fx.disclaimer: Indicative rate — on-chain amounts are always USDC.

docs.title: How Rota works
docs.subtitle: A plain-language guide to saving in a circle — where your money sits, what the rules are, and what to do at every step.
docs.introP1: Rota is the savings circle your community already trusts — somiti, chit fund, tanda, susu — rebuilt so the money can never disappear. A group of 3–20 people contributes a fixed amount of digital dollars (USDC) every round, and each round one member takes the whole pot. After a full cycle, everyone has had their turn.
docs.introP2: The difference from a traditional circle: no person ever holds the money. A smart contract — a small program on a public blockchain — escrows every contribution, pays out exactly by the rules everyone agreed to, and records every step publicly. Nobody, not even the organizer, can change the rules or run away with the pot.
docs.startIntro: You need about five minutes and a browser wallet. No bank account, no paperwork.
docs.startStep1Desc: Install MetaMask (or any compatible wallet) and press “Connect wallet” in the top-right corner. Rota will offer to add the right network for you.
docs.startStep2Desc: USDC is a digital dollar — 1 USDC is designed to always be worth $1. While Rota is on testnet, get free test USDC from the faucet; it also covers your (tiny) network fees.
docs.startStep3Desc: Start your own circle with a two-minute wizard, or open an invite link from someone you know and join with one tap. Joining escrows your collateral — it comes back when the circle completes.
docs.startStep4Title: Contribute each round, take the pot on your turn
docs.startStep4Desc: The dashboard and reminders tell you exactly what to do and when. Everything else — payouts, records, refunds — the contract handles by itself.
docs.productCircleDesc: The classic rotation. Every member pays the same amount each round; one member takes the whole pot, in a fixed order or an order drawn at the start. Simple and predictable.
docs.productCircleBest: Best for: familiar somiti-style saving with family or coworkers.
docs.productBidDesc: Like a chit fund. Members who haven't taken a pot yet can bid a discount to take it early. The discount is shared with all members as a dividend — patience literally pays.
docs.productBidBest: Best for: groups where someone sometimes needs money sooner.
docs.productPotDesc: No rotation — everyone saves toward a shared target (a wedding, tuition, Eid, land). The pot stays locked until the target or the deadline, then each member withdraws exactly what they put in.
docs.productPotBest: Best for: saving toward one goal, together, without temptation.
docs.lifecycleIntro: Every circle moves through the same simple phases. You can always see the current phase at the top of the circle page.
docs.phaseOpenDesc: The organizer shares the invite link. Each member joins by depositing collateral. If the circle doesn't fill by its deadline, everyone reclaims their collateral — no harm done.
docs.phaseActiveDesc: Once every seat is filled, anyone can start the circle. The payout order is fixed (or drawn on-chain). Each round, every member contributes before the round deadline.
docs.phaseSettleDesc: When everyone has paid — or the deadline passes — anyone can press “Settle round”. The contract pays the round's recipient, covers any missed payment from that member's collateral, and opens the next round.
docs.phaseDoneDesc: After the last round, members withdraw their collateral (and any dividends). Every completed circle strengthens your Credit Passport.
docs.bidP1: In a Bid Circle, the first part of each round is an auction. Members who haven't taken a pot yet can bid the share of the pot they're willing to give up to take it now. The highest discount wins; if nobody bids, the pot simply goes by join order.
docs.bidExample: Ten members save 100 USDC a round, so the pot is 1,000 USDC. Rahima needs cash now and bids a 10% discount. She wins and receives 900 USDC. The 100 USDC discount is split among all ten members — everyone else earns 10 USDC just for waiting.
docs.bidP2: The organizer caps the maximum discount (up to 30%) when creating the circle, so nobody can be squeezed in a desperate moment. Dividends collect in the contract and can be withdrawn at any time.
docs.collateralP1: When you join a circle, you deposit collateral — typically one round's contribution. It's your seat deposit and the group's safety net, and it's returned in full when the circle completes.
docs.collateralP2: If a member misses a round, nobody else has to cover them: the contract takes the missing amount from that member's collateral, so the recipient still gets a full pot. The missed round is recorded on the member's Credit Passport.
docs.collateralP3: A member in default can make it right at any time by “curing”: repaying what their collateral covered plus a 5% penalty. That restores their standing and their right to a payout.
docs.autopayP1: AutoPay makes sure you never miss a round by accident. When you opt in, anyone — a family member, the organizer, or a bot — can trigger your contribution for you when it's due. Handy when you're travelling or offline.
docs.autopayP2: It is strictly limited: the contract can only pull your exact contribution amount, only once per round, only into your own circle, and only while you stay opted in. Your money is never in anyone's custody, and you can switch AutoPay off at any time.
docs.givingP1: A circle can optionally route a fixed share of every pot to a charity address chosen at creation. The share and the recipient are locked into the contract and shown on the circle page, so giving is transparent and automatic — no one has to remember, and no one can divert it.
docs.passportP1: Every on-time contribution, completed circle, and cured default builds a public score tied to your address — like a credit history for people banks have never seen. Missed rounds and early exits lower it.
docs.passportP2: Your passport page is shareable: show it to a lender, a landlord, or your next circle. Anyone can verify it directly on-chain, and you can prove the address is yours with one free signature.
docs.genlayerP1: The News page has no editor and no backend server — it is curated by an Intelligent Contract on the GenLayer testnet. On every refresh, independent validators browse live news feeds, ask an AI to pick and summarize the most relevant stories for the Arc ecosystem and the world of group savings, and reach consensus on the result before storing it on-chain.
docs.genlayerP2: That makes the feed transparent and verifiable: anyone can read the contract, trigger a refresh, and see exactly what the validators agreed on. It is the same trust model as Rota's savings contracts, applied to information.
docs.contractsIntro: Rota is fully on-chain and open source. Hover over any contract and click through to inspect it in the explorer yourself.
docs.feeRotaDesc: The protocol takes no cut of contributions, pots, or dividends. 100% of what the circle saves goes to its members (minus any giving share the circle itself chose).
docs.feeGasDesc: Each action (joining, contributing, settling) is a blockchain transaction on Arc. Fees are paid in USDC, and a typical transaction costs around $0.01.
docs.feeFxDesc: Taka, rupee, or peso amounts are indicative conversions to help you think in your own currency. The money that moves on-chain is always exactly the USDC amount shown.
docs.faqQ1: Can the organizer take the money?
docs.faqA1: No. The organizer sets the rules and invites people, but contributions go straight into the smart contract. Payouts follow the fixed rules; the organizer has no withdraw button and no special access to funds.
docs.faqQ2: What happens if someone doesn't pay?
docs.faqA2: The contract covers the missing amount from that member's collateral, so the round's recipient is still paid in full. The defaulter's score drops, and they can cure the default (repay plus 5%) to restore their standing.
docs.faqQ3: Can I leave once a circle has started?
docs.faqA3: An active circle runs to completion — that's the promise members make each other, and collateral backs it. Goal Pots are different: you can leave early, at the cost of a penalty that is shared among the members who stay.
docs.faqA4: USDC is a widely used “stablecoin” issued by Circle: a digital token designed to always be redeemable one-for-one for US dollars. Rota uses it so a circle spread across Dhaka, Dubai, and London saves in one stable unit.
docs.faqQ5: Do I need to understand crypto?
docs.faqA5: You need a wallet app, and that's it. If you can use bKash or WhatsApp, you can use Rota: the app tells you what to do each round, shows amounts in your own currency, and explains every action before you confirm it.
docs.faqQ6: Is this real money right now?
docs.faqA6: Not yet. Rota runs on Arc testnet with free test USDC, so you can try every feature end-to-end without risking anything. Real-money launch waits for security audits and market-by-market legal review.
docs.faqQ7: What if I lose my wallet?
docs.faqA7: Your wallet's recovery phrase is the only key to your funds and your passport — Rota has no server, no account, and no way to reset it for you. Write the phrase down and keep it somewhere safe before joining a circle.
docs.safetyIntro: Rota is built to be trustworthy, and part of that is being clear about what it doesn't solve:
docs.safetyItem1: Collateral covers one missed round, not many. Choose members you actually know — the contract enforces rules, but a good circle is still built on real trust.
docs.safetyItem2: The contracts are open source and heavily tested, but not yet professionally audited. That's one of the reasons Rota stays on testnet.
docs.safetyItem3: Random payout order uses on-chain randomness that is theoretically influenceable by network validators; a stronger randomness source is planned before mainnet.
docs.safetyItem4: Pooled savings are regulated differently in every country. Rota will only launch with real money where that has been properly reviewed.
docs.roadmapIntro: Rota is built in the open, in deliberate phases — and real money only arrives after the safety work is done. Here is the honest sequence:
docs.roadmapPhase1Desc: All three products, collateral and AutoPay, the Credit Passport, English and Bangla interfaces, and the AI news desk are live on Arc testnet — anyone can try everything with free test USDC.
docs.roadmapPhase2Desc: Stronger randomness for random-order circles, deeper collateral options, rewards for settling rounds on time, farm-resistant passport scores — and a professional security audit of the contracts.
docs.roadmapPhase3Desc: Urdu, Hindi, Spanish, and Tagalog interfaces, Telegram deadline reminders, funding a circle straight from USDC on other chains, and a shareable soul-bound Credit Passport.
docs.roadmapPhase4Desc: Mainnet launch begins with a small, supervised pilot of circles in Bangladesh — strictly after the security audit is complete and each market's regulations have been reviewed.
docs.roadmapPhase5Desc: In-app bKash/Nagad, UPI, and GCash on-ramps, a mobile app, emergency micro-advances backed by your passport, and an AI assistant for organizers.
docs.roadmapNote: The detailed engineering roadmap — with phase gates, metrics, and the risk register — lives in the project repository (docs/ROADMAP.md).
docs.ctaDesc: Create a test circle, invite a friend or two, and play a few rounds — it's the fastest way to understand Rota.

news.title: News
news.subtitle: The best of the Arc ecosystem and the world of group savings — found, ranked, and summarized on-chain by an AI intelligent contract.
news.poweredBy: Curated by an Intelligent Contract on the GenLayer Studio testnet — validators browse the news and reach consensus on the best stories, powered by
news.relevance: Relevance, as scored by the curator
news.refreshing: Curating… validators are reading the news (30–60s)
news.refreshFailed: On-chain refresh failed. The Studio testnet may be busy — try again in a minute.
news.refreshHint: Asks the GenLayer contract to fetch the latest feeds and re-curate
news.contractAddress: Intelligent contract address on GenLayer studionet
news.loadErrorHint: The GenLayer Studio testnet may be down or the contract address may be stale. Check VITE_GENLAYER_NEWS_CONTRACT.
news.emptyHint: The contract is deployed but hasn't curated anything. Trigger the first on-chain refresh.
news.setupBody: This page is powered by a GenLayer Intelligent Contract that browses the web and curates news by validator consensus. Deploy it once, then point the app at it:

errors.AlreadyInitialized: This contract is already set up.
errors.InvalidParams: Some settings are invalid — please review the form.
errors.WrongPhase: This action isn't available in the circle's current state.
errors.NotMember: You're not a member of this circle.
errors.NotInvited: This circle is invite-only and your address isn't on the list.
errors.CircleNotFull: The circle can't start until every seat is filled.
errors.RoundClosed: This round's contribution window has closed.
errors.BidTooLow: Your bid must beat the current best bid.
errors.BidTooHigh: That bid exceeds the maximum discount.
errors.NotEligibleToBid: You can't bid — you've already received a pot or are in default.
errors.TooEarlyToSettle: The round can't be settled yet.
errors.NotOrganizer: Only the organizer can do that.
errors.OpenDeadlineNotReached: The fill deadline hasn't passed yet.
errors.PotFull: This pot has reached its member limit.
errors.CannotUnlockYet: The pot can't unlock yet — target not reached and deadline not passed.
errors.ERC20InsufficientBalance: Not enough USDC in your wallet.
errors.ERC20InsufficientAllowance: Approval too low — approve USDC first.
errors.InsufficientGas: Not enough gas funds in your wallet.
errors.WrongNetwork: Your wallet is on the wrong network.
errors.Unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.

tour.welcomeBody: Rota is the savings circle your community already trusts — somiti, tanda, susu — run by a smart contract, so nobody can run away with the pot. This one-minute tour shows you around.
tour.connectBody: Everything starts here. Connect MetaMask (or any browser wallet) — Rota adds the right network for you. Once connected, open this menu to get free test USDC.
tour.dashboardBody: Every circle and goal pot you join lives on the dashboard. Colored badges tell you exactly when something needs you — a contribution due, a bid window open, funds ready to withdraw.
tour.createTitle: Start a circle or goal pot
tour.createBody: A two-minute wizard: name it, set the amount and rules, then share the invite link on WhatsApp. Your people join with one tap.
tour.passportBody: Every on-time contribution and completed circle builds a public, on-chain credit score you own — portable proof of savings discipline you can show a lender or landlord.
tour.notificationsBody: Reminders collect here: contributions due, bid windows closing, funds ready to withdraw. You can also turn on browser notifications.
tour.languageBody: Switch the interface language and show amounts in taka, rupees, or pesos. The money that moves on-chain is always exactly the USDC shown.
tour.docsBody: The Guide explains everything in plain language — collateral, bidding, AutoPay, fees — in about five minutes. Enjoy Rota: your turn always comes.
